title: afzelia acuminata
intro: Afzelia acuminata, also known as the Indian lilac or wrightia acuminata, is a tropical plant species native to India and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and exhibiting antiseptic properties, making it a valuable herb in Ayurvedic medicine. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its extracts are also used in the production of medicinal oils and ointments.
